fieldValues.value: Structured filters and contentMatch are conjunctive; structured filters cannot be overridden by content-match recall

When both structured filters and contentMatch are specified in a DiscoveryQuery, an instance MUST satisfy all specified structured filter predicates (typeId, typeNamespace, typeName, containerId, tag, lifecycleState, tier) AND the contentMatch recall-floor predicate. The structured-filter conditions impose an exact inclusion/exclusion constraint. An instance that matches contentMatch but fails a structured filter predicate MUST NOT appear in the result set.

Structured and content predicates belong to different regimes: structured filters are deterministic and exact; content matching is a probabilistic floor. The conjunction rule prevents content-match extra recall from leaking instances that should be excluded by a type or container filter — which would make the type filter semantically meaningless.
